cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this problem step by step. So, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2000, and today is her 16th birthday. We need to find the date one week from today in MM/DD/YYYY format. The options are given, so I need to figure out which one is correct. First, let me clarify a few things. The last day of February is usually the 28th, unless it's a leap year, in which case it's the 29th. Since 2000 is a leap year (divisible by 400), February had 29 days. So Jane's birthday is February 29, 2000. Now, today is her 16th birthday. But here's a catch: February 29th only occurs every four years. So if today is her 16th birthday, does that mean it's 2016 (since 16 years after 2000 is 2016), and this year is a leap year (2016 is divisible by 4 and not by 100 unless also by 400)? Let's check: 2016 is a leap year. So her 16th birthday would be on February 29, 2016. That makes sense because each leap year birthday would count as one year. So in 2000: 0,
